visa_policy.notes: All nationalities listed receive a visitor visa issued on arrival (gratis, extendable up to 6 months) rather than a pre-cleared visa-free entry; the official Tonga Embassy page classifies them as 'visa waiver' countries. Schengen Area passport holders (27 EU member states plus Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland) receive 90 days; UAE nationals receive 60 days; China receives 30 days; all other waiver-list countries receive 31 days. Nationalities not on the official list must obtain a letter of authority before travelling and apply for a visitor visa on arrival. An official e-visa portal exists at evisa.gov.to but was unreachable during this validation — eligibility scope unconfirmed. Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, and Switzerland are Schengen Area members but not EU member states.

cbi.notes: As of June 2026 no official CBI legislation has been enacted. A proposal reportedly designed by Henley & Partners was publicly backed by PM Lord Fakafanua in December 2025 (cited investment threshold approximately USD 190,000 single / USD 220,000 family), but it has not been legislated, formally approved, or launched. No official government URL for any enacted CBI program exists. Sources for the proposal are news/aggregator only and have not been recorded.

programs.name: Residency Visa
programs.notes: Available to persons with Tongan ancestry, former Tongan citizenship, or marriage to a Tongan national. Not an investment program. Fee: USD 115 (adults), USD 54.50 (minors). No investment amount required. Duration and path to PR not specified on official page.

programs.name: Assured Income Visa
programs.notes: For non-Tongan passport holders seeking retirement in Tonga. Requires proof of minimum annual income of TOP 10,000 from an overseas pension or fund. No capital/property investment required. Application fee: USD 345. Visa valid for 2 years or more, renewable. Work, study, and business activities are prohibited. Overstay penalty: TOP 1,500. Not an investment program but a passive-income residency pathway.

agreements: PACER Plus (Pacific Agreement on Closer Economic Relations Plus) — Tonga is a party; entered into force 13 December 2020; covers temporary movement of natural persons among parties (Australia, Cook Islands, Kiribati, New Zealand, Niue, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Tonga, Tuvalu, Vanuatu)

gaps: evisa.gov.to timed out again during this validation — could not confirm whether the e-visa portal covers different nationalities than the on-arrival visa waiver system or is even operational
revenue.gov.to SSL certificate error prevents direct scrape; data sourced from the official Tonga Embassy in Beijing site
Exact list of countries requiring a pre-travel letter of authority (i.e., those not on the waiver list) not published on any official page found

Israel bilateral agreement (in force April 2017): Tonga Embassy page lists Israel in the standard 31-day waiver group; Wikipedia claims 90 days — no official Tonga government page confirms 90 days for Israel, so 31 days (per embassy page) is retained
Path to PR or citizenship via Residency Visa or Assured Income Visa not specified on official pages
CBI program proposed but not yet legislated — no official URL to record; proposal details from news sources only
No digital nomad, startup, or fast-track skilled-worker visa confirmed on any official Tonga government page

No official Tonga government page found granting entry benefits based on holding a third-country credential (US visa, Schengen visa, UK visa, residence permit, or permanent residence). conditional_access set to empty accordingly.
No OCI cardholder conditional entry rules found for Tonga from any official source
validation_notes: CORRECTED: UAE max_stay_days changed from 31 to 60 — bilateral MOU entered into force 24 May 2018 explicitly grants 60 days per visit, confirmed by Gulf News article citing the UAE-Tonga agreement
CORRECTED: China max_stay_days changed from 31 to 30 — the 2016 bilateral ordinary-passport visa waiver agreement specifies 30 days, not 31

CORRECTED: Notes for Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, and Switzerland updated from 'Schengen member state' to 'Schengen Area member (non-EU)' — these four countries are in the Schengen Area but are not EU member states
CORRECTED: Ireland note clarified to state Ireland is not part of the Schengen Area and receives 31 days (not 90 days)
VERIFIED: UAE note updated to include MOU date (April 2018, force 24 May 2018) and that all passport types are covered
VERIFIED: Bulgaria and Romania Schengen accession notes added (Bulgaria October 2024, Romania April 2024)
VERIFIED: Israel 31 days retained per official embassy page; Wikipedia's claim of 90 days not supported by any official Tonga government source
VERIFIED: Assured Income Visa overstay penalty (TOP 1,500) added to notes from official page
VERIFIED: CBI program remains unenacted as of June 2026; proposal status unchanged
VERIFIED: RBI programs (Residency Visa and Assured Income Visa) confirmed against official embassy pages with no changes to amounts

outbound_official.note: No official Tonga government outbound visa-list page located. The tongaembassycn.gov.to page briefly notes Schengen visa-free access for Tongan passport holders (as of November 2015) within an inbound-focused page, but no dedicated government page analogous to India's mea.gov.in/vffin exists on any gov.to domain. mfa.gov.to is unreachable (TLS errors). revenue.gov.to covers only inbound requirements. Rely on destination-side data.
